column1: Instructions:  Save a copy of this file with a new name in the directory hpchem\n\sequence, where n is your instrument number (typically 1),
then edit your new file in Excel or other appropriate environment to create a .CSV file for import to a ChemStation Standard Sequence Table.
Your .CSV file MUST contain a bracketed Table section identifier immediately prior to the start of the data section.  Also, the Sequence Table column headers
must be spelled exactly as listed in this default file, unless aliases are used (see the Import CSV HELP in ChemStation for more details about aliases.)
Correct header spellings are also available from the "stabhdr.txt" file located in the path "hpchem/ms".
Although this default CSV file contains column headers for all of the ChemStation Standard Sequence Table columns, it is not required
that your .CSV file for Standard Sequence import contain all of the Standard Sequence Table columns; any subset is also acceptable.
NOTE:  Rows 1 through 15 in this default file can be deleted when you prepare your own .CSV import file.  They are only for reference use.
Lines 16 and 17 can also be deleted or overwritten, but please take note of the requirement for the bracketed Table section identifier, and also the requirement for
correct column header spellings, when preparing your .CSV sequence import file.
